leadText: The Escape Rope (Japanese: あなぬけのヒモ Hole-Escape Rope) is a type of item introduced in Generation I. It is used to escape from a cave or a dungeon. In most games, it is a consumable item; in Pokémon Sword and Shield, it is a Key Item. It was developed by Silph Co.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Manual activation 1.1.1 Generation I 1.1.2 Generation II-VII 1.1.3 Generation VIII 1.2 Exchange 1.3 Other uses Effect [ edit | edit source ] Manual activation [ edit | edit source ] Generation I [ edit | edit source ] When used in the overworld while the player is in a cave or dungeon, the player is warped to the last Pokémon Center they healed at (or the player's house if they have never healed at a Pokémon Center). Due to an oversight in Pokémon Red and Blue , this can be used to escape from from the Sea Cottage or the Vermilion City Pokémon Fan Club . This was fixed in Pokémon Yellow . Generation II-VII [ edit | edit source ] When used in the overworld while the player is in a cave or dungeon, the player is warped to the entrance through which the player entered the cave or dungeon. It cannot be used in Pokémon Colosseum and XD: Gale of Darkness . In Pokémon Ruby, Sapphire , and Emerald , if the player uses the Escape Rope to exit from Inside of Truck at the start of the game before the truck has left (even though this item is not legitimately available at this point), the player will warp to the front of the southwestern house in Petalburg City and the truck will remain in Littleroot Town . If the player exits any building in this town while the truck is still there, a glitched version of the animation where the player leaves the truck will start. The Escape Rope cannot be used to escape from Turnback Cave . Spear Pillar , the Distortion World , and the Hall of Origin are not treated as caves. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Sword and Shield, the Escape Rope is now an infinite-use Key Item . In Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl , it is a consumable item again. Exchange [ edit | edit source ] In Anville Town , on weekends the player can give a man two Escape Ropes in exchange for a Revive . Other uses [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Crystal and HeartGold and SoulSilver , in the Ruins of Alph , the player requires an Escape Rope to open the hidden room in the northeastern chamber. In Pokémon Crystal, the item is consumed and the player escapes as normal. In Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver, the item is not consumed and the player does not move.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad When in a place like a cave, this returns you to the last Pok é mon Center visited. G S C Use for escaping from caves, etc. R S E Use to escape instantly from a cave or a dungeon. Colo XD An item brought over from a faraway place. FR LG D P Pt HG SS B W B2 W2 A long, durable rope. Use it to escape instantly from a cave or a dungeon. X Y OR AS S M US UM A long and durable rope. Use it to escape instantly from a cave or a dungeon. P E BD SP A long and durable rope. Use it to escape instantly from locations like caves or dungeons. Sw Sh LA S V A long and durable rope. Use it to escape instantly from locations like caves or dungeons. It can be used any number of times.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen , it is possible to use an Escape Rope to escape from the Saffron Gym . This is the only Gym in these games where this is possible. In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , this item is labeled as a key item, despite being completely unused.
